summary.what: Migrate beads from server-mode Dolt + JSONL-in-git transport to embedded Dolt + canonical refs/dolt/data sync
summary.why: The repo was in a broken hybrid state — server-mode Dolt couldn't persist under the sandbox (re-imported JSONL every call), a stale CLI remote + 3-week-old refs/dolt/data existed while steering claimed 'no remote', so aligning to the canonical embedded model (matching osprey-strike) was the only coherent fix
summary.how: Stopped/killed the server, set aside the stale server DB + backup dir, flipped metadata.json to embedded, bd bootstrap rebuilt embeddeddolt from the trusted 60-issue JSONL; dropped the stale refs/dolt/data and re-seeded it fresh; flipped export.auto/git-add false, untracked + gitignored issues.jsonl, rewrote AGENTS.md sync sections; verified via fresh-clone bd bootstrap round-trip

notes: JSONL was the only current source of truth (server Dolt last modified Mar 22; refs/dolt/data topped out May 7), so rebuild-from-JSONL was safe and correct despite losing Dolt audit history. Followed osprey-strike's validated migration doc as the playbook but adapted for the server->embedded delta osprey didn't have (osprey was already embedded). bd dolt set has no mode key — the switch is a metadata.json edit (gitignored→ wait, tracked here) + bd bootstrap. Bootstrap detection order matters: had to drop refs/dolt/data AND set aside .beads/backup so it fell through to the current JSONL instead of cloning stale state. Hand-edits to the Sync/Landing-the-Plane sections landed inside bd's managed markers (timbers-069 tracks relocating them). Kept JSONL as /tmp backup throughout.
